title: Rift imposter
description: A Rift / Mountebank-compatible imposter document (POST /imposters body) with zio-bdd Rift's `_rift.*` extensions. Used by NativeSpec.Rift(imposterJson) and raw MockSource files.

port.description: TCP port to bind. 0 (or omitted) asks Rift for an ephemeral port.

protocol.description: Wire protocol served by this imposter.

name.description: Human-readable label for the imposter (shown in logs / the Rift UI).

recordRequests.description: When true, Rift records matched requests for later inspection (received/verify).

defaultResponse.description: Response served when no stub matches. zio-bdd pins this to { statusCode: 404 } for cross-adapter parity.

stubs.description: Ordered list of stubs; the first whose predicates all match wins.

predicates.description: Predicates ANDed together; the stub matches only when all pass.

responses.description: Responses served in round-robin as the stub matches repeatedly.

scenarioName.description: _rift declarative-scenario FSM name (rift#190). The stub is eligible only in the matching scenario state.

requiredScenarioState.description: State the (flow_id, scenarioName) FSM must be in for this stub to be eligible.

newScenarioState.description: State to transition to after serving. Omit to stay in the current state.

predicate.description: A Mountebank predicate. Operator keys (equals, deepEquals, contains, matches, startsWith, endsWith, exists) map request fields (method, path, query, headers, body); and/or/not compose sub-predicates.

response.description: A stub response: `is` (canned), `proxy`, or `inject`, optionally with Mountebank `_behaviors` and Rift `_rift` extensions.

scripts.description: Named script registry (Rift 0.13). Each entry is referenced by name from a response via `_rift.script.ref`. Registry entries use `code` or `file` (a `ref` may not chain to another `ref`).

flowState.description: Backs declarative scenarios (rift#190): where per-flow FSM state lives and how a request's flow id is derived.

ttlSeconds.description: How long idle flow state is retained.

flowIdSource.description: How the flow id is resolved: "imposter_port" (PerInstance) or "header:<name>" (Correlated).

riftScriptSource.description: A script's source and engine (Rift 0.13). Exactly one of `code` or `file` must be set; `engine` is optional when it can be inferred from a `file` extension.

engine.description: Scripting engine. Optional — inferred from a `file` extension (.rhai → rhai, .js → javascript) when omitted.

file.description: Path to a script file, resolved relative to the configfile/datadir (admin-API imposters resolve under --scripts-dir). Engine inferred from the extension.

riftScript.description: A response script: inline `code`, a `file`, or a `ref` to an entry in the imposter's `_rift.scripts` registry — exactly one must be set.

engine.description: Scripting engine. Optional — inferred from a `file` extension (.rhai → rhai, .js → javascript) when omitted.

file.description: Path to a script file; engine inferred from the extension.

ref.description: Name of a script registered under the imposter's `_rift.scripts`. A `ref` may not point at another `ref`.

script.description: Dynamic response via a Rhai (built-in) or JavaScript (Boa) script (Rift 0.13). The script defines `respond(ctx)` — or a bare-expression body — returning a result constructor (`http(...)`, `delay(...)`, `pass()`, ...).

fault.description: Fault injection (rift#239). `tcp` aborts the connection at the transport layer; `latency` sleeps then serves the response.
